free_text: Light charged particle emission induced by fast
neutrons (25 to 65 MeV) on Co-59
Benck, Slypen, Meulders, Raeymackers, Nica
Corcalciuc, Nica
Data in figs. Total
production cr. sect. data for 25 to 62-MeV energy
Final publ., data in Tables for
natFe, 59Co, 209Bi, and natU.
Description of the
experimental arrangement
Analysis details
- thick target
correction procedure by Monte Carlo simulation program
Description of the
CYCLONE facility
Fast neutron facility cyclotron
'CYCLONE'
65 MeV proton beam strikes 3 mm thick
natural lithium target. Proton current is 10 micro-A
Well-defined peak at 62.7 MeV,full width at half
maximum is 2 MeV containing 50% of neutrons, plus
a flat continuum of low-energy neutrons.
-For energy calibration the proton and deuteron recoi-
ling from respectively a polypropylene (1 mm thick)
and deuterated polypropylene target (0.6 mm thick)
were used.
- neutron flux about 1.E+5 neutrons/cm2/sec
Cross sections were measured
at degrees between 20 and 70 degree with step 10 deg.
Four charged particles detector
telescopes were used simultaneously. Each of them
consists of
1.  'DELTA-E' detector Ne102 plastic scintillator,
0.1 mm thick, 4 cm diameter, and
2.   Cs-I(Tl) 'E' detector, crystal 22 mm thick,
38.1 mm diameter
The time-of-flight method was deduced from the
time difference between the capacitive beam pickoff
located upstream from the neutron-producing target,
and the delta-e detector. Only those events of
charged particles detection  were registered which
are associated with neutrons in the main neutron
peak.
.Elemental cobalt target of surface 6 * 6 cm2 and
0.4 mm thick
Used semiclassical exciton model including
Hauser-Feshbach statistical theory
Total error includes statistical error and
systematic error (ERR-SYS)
Error of the absolute scale of energy
spectrum includes:
Beam monitoring error
Solid angle correction error
Error due to number of sample nuclei
Error of the standard H(n,p) cross section
Statist. error of recoil protons peak,etc
* EN-RSL - half width of the incident neuron energy bin
The uncertainties of the extrapolated values are
estimated to be 10% for protons, 25% for deuterons,
30% for tritons, and 20% for alpha particles. They are
based on the general agreement between the measured
and the calculated spectra or on the extrapolation
procedure.
Final DDX, DA, DE data are from Tbl.2 of
AND,87,231,2004. Integral cr.sect. are from
JP/G,28,2823,2002. Prelim.data were obtained through
Internet and approved 2003-09, Sylvie Benck.
Data are almost the same.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Ref. on AND,87,231,2004 was added.
Derived data lines (marked by FLAG) were moved to
separated REACTION (multiple reaction formalism used)
039-044, 046. ERR-ANALYS update. Data updated based on
last publ. at AND,87,231,2004. All values=0. without
error are deleted following Tbl.2 of AND,87,231,2004
and confirmation from Dr.S.Benck.
Proton production
DDX cross sections

interpolation of near-lying values
SD: Derived data lines (marked by FLAG)
were moved to separated REACTION (multiple reaction
formalism used). A few values are slightly corrected
and part of data with values=0. deleted following
Tbl.2 of AND,87,231,2004. A few values at E=53.MeV
were corrected: at 120. degr. (0.0 -> 0.001), at
130. and 140. degr. deleted following Dr.S.Benck remark
At E=55.MeV three values are corrected following
presentation in the AND,87,231,2004. E-RSL deleted
(wrong code was used). EN-RSL-FW -> EN-RSL.
